reasoning:
The target threshold is the March 28 close price (approximately $213.19). RL has seen an 8% drop in the last week alone, culminating in a heavy selloff on Friday, March 28. This recent drop was catalyzed by sector-wide consumer softness concerns (spurred by a disappointing update and guidance miss from Lululemon) and a hot PCE inflation reading that rattled the broader market. 

In the immediate short term (April 6 to April 29), momentum is strongly negative. The stock broke down below its pattern’s lower trendline with surging volume, indicating bearish sentiment that often persists in the 1-to-4 week timeframe as analysts revise their ratings based on peer earnings read-across. Therefore, I estimate the probability of a quick recovery above $213.19 to be slightly below 50% (0.46 - 0.48).

In the medium-to-long term, negative momentum typically fades. RL maintains strong fundamentals—beating top- and bottom-line expectations and raising guidance as recently as February 2025, buoyed by international tailwinds and share buybacks. Because the threshold is set at a depressed bottom following a sharp selloff, the long-term positive equity drift gradually shifts the probability back toward 50% and marginally higher (0.49 - 0.51) for the 3-, 6-, and 12-month marks. All estimates are intentionally kept close to 0.5 because directional stock pricing over these horizons is predominantly a random walk.
